curious.title: Sacrena — competitive analysis

projects.name: Primal Ops

projects.note: New engagement: operations + data platform for Primal Moves Venice. Distinct from PRIMAL (member-facing Flutter app, parked) and primal-agent (AI voice agent, mock-mode). Repo `primal-ops` scaffolded today at giddyuptiger/primal-ops with the MVP cut: MVP-1 Mindbody → Klaviyo email flows, MVP-2 Toast + Mindbody joined member profile, MVP-3 cafe-credit triggers, + MVP-4 influencer affiliate program (codes like MIKI25, landing pages at /r/<CODE>, attribution funnel, multi-rail payouts via Stripe Connect / Mindbody / Toast / free month, per-influencer dashboard). Affiliate slots in as Phase 1.5, 3-5 days on top of Phase 1, architecturally a sibling of the cafe-credit triggers (same Supabase schema, same rule engine, different reward type). Source docs (MVP plan, meeting notes, ops proposal, engagement terms Option C, AI agent build spec, MVP-4 affiliate supplement) archived in docs/. With AI-assisted build the original 3-week Phase 1 compresses to ~1 week once access lands.
projects.nextStep: Send `primal-ops/docs/06_Week1_Kickoff_Email.md` to Primal. Asking for Mindbody admin, Toast admin + tier, Klaviyo access, front-desk number provider, and the top refund/freeze/cancel policies — items 1-3 unblock Week 1 build. Email now also includes the MVP-4 affiliate program scope; on the kickoff call also lock the 3-5 seed influencer names + the default discount/payout terms.
projects.playbook:
**Two-hour Primal discovery sprint — produces real numbers for pricing.**

### 0-30 min · Klaviyo audit
- Log in. List every existing flow Primal has. Note which are working, which are stubs.
- Look at existing campaigns. Are templates usable as-is, or do they need a rebuild?
- Check Klaviyo's Mindbody integration in their app library. If it exists and works, you might be able to skip the custom sync entirely.

### 30-60 min · Mindbody ↔ Klaviyo connection path
- Open Klaviyo Integrations → search Mindbody. If there's a native integration, test it on a sample list.
- If no native: try Zapier as the second option (~$50/mo, fast to wire). Set up one trigger end-to-end to see latency + reliability.
- If neither: you build the sync. Note which API endpoints you'd hit (Mindbody Public API: clients, visits, classes).

### 60-90 min · Toast audit
- Confirm Primal's Toast product tier. Open the Toast API docs for that tier.
- Specifically check: is there an endpoint for issuing customer credit programmatically? If yes, cafe credit is easy. If no, fall back to email-delivered coupon codes redeemable in Toast (still works, slightly more manual).

### 90-120 min · Convert findings into a real Phase 1 estimate + your gut
- Klaviyo flows recreate: honest hours
- Mindbody sync: honest hours based on which path worked
- Toast cafe credit: honest hours

Multiply honest hours by honest rate. That's your Phase 1 price. Use it as the anchor for the full proposal.

### Three possible outcomes
- **If Phase 1 is 8-12h (Klaviyo native + Mindbody integration works):** Drop Phase 1 fee to $1,000, ship in days, build credibility, hand them an updated rest-of-build proposal.
- **If it's ~25-35h as originally estimated:** Your Option C numbers were sound. Send Option B ($15k base + 25% rev share 18mo).
- **If it's worse (Mindbody API gated, Toast doesn't expose credit issuance):** Proposal stays, flag the integration risk and price in margin. $18k base instead of $15k.

### After the sprint
Come back with real numbers. I'll have the pricing agreement PDF in your style drafted within five minutes of you giving me the verdict.

projects.name: GRDN
projects.shortStatus: iOS exit-65 fix + Live Activity fix pushed (build 1.11.5+5); web live
projects.note: iOS: exit-65 archive fix shipped (build 1.11.3+4) and the 10-min Gate Pause Live Activity dismissal bug fixed (build 1.11.5+5, commit b392cd6). Root cause of the Live Activity bug: dismissal timers ran in the Dart isolate, but iOS suspends Flutter during the 10-min unlock window so the timers never fired. Fix hands the dismissal deadline to iOS itself via `.after(endDate)` policy through a small native Swift bridge, so iOS dismisses the activity on time regardless of Flutter's state. Web live at grdnapp.com. Home-screen redesign spec is delivered.
projects.nextStep: Re-run the GRDN Xcode Cloud build (now also picks up the Live Activity fix). Home-screen: run the Stitch prompt, pick a design, then Claude can build the redesigned home screen.

projects.name: SCANDALS
projects.shortStatus: v1.10.0 shipped; consolidated 13-point + width markers task running
projects.note: v1.10.0 shipped: single-photo scan, optional flash toggle, and a reworked toe profile that traces the front foot edge in the full-resolution photo so the five toe-tip peaks resolve cleanly even when toes touch. A consolidated task is now landing the rest of the visual rework: 10% width-station markers on the outline (measured vs extrapolated styled differently), the 13-point export model (5 toe tips + inside-big-toe + 2 edges at 1/3 cross-section + 2 edges at 2/3 cross-section + 3 heel points) for Grasshopper, removing the obsolete ball/arch/heel key, removing the bogus heel-alignment warning (the calf obscures the heel so the program can't actually judge it), and fixing the 0 mm heel-extrapolation glitch.
projects.nextStep: Consolidated SCANDALS task in flight. After it lands, take a real scan to verify the 13-point output and the cleaner visual.

projects.name: Maestro
projects.note: Personal AI manager system. Calendar wired, goals seeded, three scheduled cadences set. Phase 2 will add pattern recognition + cross-project tradeoff surfacing + tunable nudge frequency.
projects.nextStep: Review CLAUDE/maestro/goals.json — replace starter goals with your real ones. Confirm assertiveness setting (default: coach).
projects.playbook:
**Maestro goals — your real ones.**

Two layers: the North Star (your overall aspiration, not directly scorable) and the active goals (the actionable paths toward it, which Maestro weights and surfaces).

### ★ North Star — Build a sustainable revenue stream
The overall direction. Doesn't get scored on its own because it isn't directly actionable. Active goals ladder up to it. Drift detection compares your time spent on revenue-laddering work vs. non-laddering work.

### Active goals

**G1 — Land Primal Moves engagement** · weight 9 · 2026-06-30 · *ladders to: revenue*
- Linked projects: Primal Ops
- Closest path to actual revenue right now.

**G2 — Launch iPhone Dock on Amazon** · weight 10 · ASAP · *ladders to: revenue*
- Linked projects: iPhone Dock
- Direct revenue-creating product launch.

**G3 — Ship 5 apps to App Store** · weight 10 · 2026-Q3 · *ladders to: revenue (long horizon)*
- Linked projects: HD-LOVE, WEATHER, GRDN, FUTON, SCANDALS
- Revenue path is longer (App Store distribution + monetization model), but each ship reduces time-to-revenue.

**G4 — Work on my relationship** · weight 9 · ongoing · *ladders to: personal wellbeing*
- Linked projects: Relationship growth
- Not revenue; intentionally first-class so it doesn't slide while revenue work dominates.

### How weights work
At brief time, each project's next action gets the weight of its goal. Among the three revenue-laddering goals, G2 (iPhone Dock ASAP) and G3 (apps Q3) tie at weight 10, with G1 (Primal) at 9. Among non-revenue, G4 sits at 9.

### Drift insights the brief surfaces
- Hours per goal per week vs. its weight
- "You spent 6h on G3 work this week but 0h on G2 — and G2 is your closest path to revenue."
- "Three days since any G4 (relationship) action. You usually touch this twice a week."

### Edit any of these
Tell me what to change. I'll update both this view AND `CLAUDE/maestro/goals.json` in the same step.
